title: Buy Misatos Cross
wikitext:
[]: Buy Misato's Cross?             Quote Post by Kenno! » Mon May 05, 2008 2:19 pm Any one know were I could get a copy of this, I know theres some out there I just can't seem to find it! And yes I have looked on Google :-) Thanks a lot people!

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Mon May 05, 2008 3:25 pm honestly, it's not that complicated. you could probably make it in about thirty minutes--maybe an hour if you're really meticulous.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon May 05, 2008 4:00 pm This isn't even something unique to Evangelion. Misato's cross is simply a white greek cross: http://www.google.com/products?q=greek+cross+necklace+plain&btnG=Search+Products&show=dd If you look around you may be able to find something similar. I think Gainax released a small silver pendant but I haven't seen it sold anywhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenno! » Mon May 05, 2008 6:37 pm mmmm I'm sure I have seen Evangelion one's though might of not been offical products then... :( Looks like i'm out of luck

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon May 05, 2008 6:42 pm http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=1869

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Mon May 05, 2008 7:17 pm Leave to zuggy to find gratuitous merchandising

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 05, 2008 7:28 pm Actually I posted that item in some other thread; it was made for Rebuild. But the thing is SO expensive: about $200 if I remember correctly. The only online store I saw it at; it was already sold out; and this was when it was still being pre-ordered only!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon May 05, 2008 7:59 pm The cross just doesn't cut it alone, you also need a huge scar to complete the look.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 05, 2008 8:41 pm Zuggy wrote: http://www.evastore.jp/index.cgi?rm=detail&id=1869

[]:             Quote Post by Kenno! » Mon May 05, 2008 11:21 pm To think it would be sooo hard for me to find a simple little cross lol I thought someone on ebay would be cranking out the fakes! I would buy the one from the eva store but I don't think they ship to the UK :-( Even if they did it cost's a looooottttt lol

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 05, 2008 11:34 pm Become friends with a jeweler.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue May 06, 2008 1:16 pm A machinist is fine, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue May 06, 2008 3:20 pm Not a bad idea, ask folks you know if they know anyone who works with metal and get in contact with them. While I was working as a watch technician the folks out back could knock something up from a chunk of metal on their lathes &ca in no time. universalperson wrote: Leave to zuggy to find gratuitous merchandising

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Wed May 07, 2008 10:47 pm Maybe my grandma could make one if I really wanted it [And I don't]. before I get mocked, let me note my grandma works with silver.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 08, 2008 1:23 am The cross always looked more like it was made of ivory (of some origin) or a synthetic substitute, rather than being a white metal -- it's too thick for a metal ornament, and is never drawn to look metallic.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu May 08, 2008 2:15 am It could even be ordinary plastic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 08, 2008 1:35 pm Reichu wrote: Looks like the bastard child of the cross from the TV show and the one from EoE. I want one that looks exactly like the close-up image in episode 12.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu May 08, 2008 4:28 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Er...wait. Meaning the cross changes it's size/design just like the Eva do? :blink:

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed May 14, 2008 11:16 pm Maybe if the woman who does Misato's Japanese voice actually wore every available cross for awhile, then maybe it'd have some legit value.

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Mon May 19, 2008 9:53 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The cross always looked more like it was made of ivory (of some origin) or a synthetic substitute, rather than being a white metal -- it's too thick for a metal ornament, and is never drawn to look metallic.

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Mon May 05, 2008 3:25 pm honestly, it's not that complicated. you could probably make it
